content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

January 9, 2014


Tom Pico
Employee Services Manager
Zone 7 Water Agency
100 North Canyons Parkway
Livermore, CA 94551

Re:     Your Request for Advice
        Our File No. A-14-190

Dear Mr. Pico:

     This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the gift provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ This letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair Political
Practices Commission does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (In re Oglesby
(1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

                                    QUESTION

     Under the Act, is the $625 lottery prize you won at the Fall Conference & Exposition of
the Association of California Water Agencies ("ACWA") a reportable or prohibited "gift"?

                                    CONCLUSION

     No. You are only responsible for reporting or prohibited from accepting gifts over a
certain amount if the source is designated in a disclosure category that applies to your position in
your agency's conflict of interest code. The ACWA is not a source that would be included in
any category applicable to your position in that code. The prize is therefore not a reportable gift
under the Act.



¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-14-190
Page No. 2

FACTS

You attended the Association of California Water Agencies' Fall Conference &
Exhibition in December, 2014. Attendance at the conference is limited to employee
representatives of member agencies. All member agencies are public water agencies in
California. About 1,800 people attended the conference and the cash drawing was open to all
attendees, which was awarded by lottery.

You work for "Zone 7 Water Agency" and attended the conference in your capacity as an
Employee Services Manager. Your disclosure categories for your agency's conflict of interest
code require you to report all interest in real property, as well as any source of income (including
gifts) or investments or business positions in business entities that:

(1) manufacture, or contract, or foreseeably contract or sell supplies, machinery or
equipment of the type utilized by the agency;
(2) perform constructions or engineering work or services of the type utilized by the
agency;
(3) manufacture or sell water-purification chemicals, supplies, equipment, machinery or
materials utilized by the agency;
(4) manufacture or sell, lease or service office equipment and supplies; motor vehicles
and parts thereof; petroleum products; data processing hardware and software;
(5) sell stocks, bonds, mutual funds and other securities; or
(6) are employment agencies or temporary help agencies.

ANALYSIS

The term "gift" is defined in Section 82028(a) as:

"Any payment that confers a personal benefit on the
recipient, to the extent that consideration of equal or
greater value is not received and includes a rebate or
discount in the price of anything of value unless the rebate
or discount is made in the regular course of business to
members of the public without regard to official status."²

Gift is also included with the definition of income. (Section 82030.)

The Act contains certain provision that require public officials to disclose certain gifts
and income and prohibit the official from accepting any such gift in excess of a specified limit,
which was $440 for 2014.³ (See Sections 87207 and 89503.) Section 87302 requires agencies to

² Section 82044 defines payment, in part, as any "rendering of . . . services or anything else of value,
whether tangible or intangible."

³ The gift limit was raised to $460 on January 1, 2015.

File No. A-14-190
Page No. 3

adopt a conflict of interest code that contains provisions insuring that officials who hold
positions that engage in the making of decisions report income/gifts from any source that may
reasonably foreseeably be materially financially affected by those decisions.

Section 89503.5(c) prohibits the acceptance of any gift in excess of $440 if the official is
required to report the source under any category applicable to the official in the official's
agency's conflict of interest code.

Because the ACWA is not a business agency designated in any of the reporting categories
applicable to your position in your agency's conflict of interest code you are neither required to report
nor prohibited from accepting the prize money you won.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Zackery P. Morazzini
General Counsel

By: Heather M. Rowan
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

HMR:jgl

whether tangible or intangible."

adopt a conflict of interest code that contains provisions insuring that officials who hold
